name: MODG (MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose) (PMC13185565)
description: MODG (MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose) — medium reported in PMC13185565 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC13185565 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.formulation_warning: workflow correction sparse; kept original — MODG is a real defined growth medium, but this paper does NOT reproduce the MOD base composition — it is cited to reference 32 (Rosenfeld E, Duport C, Zigha A, Schmitt P. 2005. Can J Microbiol 51:149-158), which is not available in this corpus. The MOD base is a chemically defined medium (mineral salts + amino acids) but its exact salt/amino-acid concentrations cannot be verified from this source, so they are not fabricated here. Only components justifiable from THIS paper are listed. Two corrections applied to the current entry: (1) oxygen was 'facultative' with EX_o2 uptake at -10 and O2 listed as a component — this is WRONG; the medium is used exclusively under strict anaerobic fermentative conditions (pO2 = 0%, continuous N2 sparging), so O2 was removed and oxygen set to 'anaerobic'; (2) glucose amount (30 mM) captured. The paper explicitly notes MOD contains NO added metal ions / iron (deliberate mild iron limitation), so no trace-metal or Fe supplement should be added. pH maintained at 7.2, 37 °C. Organism: Bacillus cereus ATCC 14579 (facultative anaerobe grown fermentatively). Full base composition should be curated directly from Rosenfeld et al. 2005 before this record is treated as complete; confidence is low precisely because the base salts/amino acids are unverifiable from this paper.
provenance.verification_evidence: grown in MOD medium supplemented with 30 mM glucose (MODG) as the carbon source (32). Anaerobic batch cultures were carried out at 37 °C ... Strict anaerobic conditions (pO2 = 0%) were achieved by continuously sparging the medium with pure nitrogen gas. // The MOD medium used in this study is not supplemented with metal ions, leading to mild iron limitation.
